business.headline: Rupee depreciation matter of concern, but no need to get alarmed about it: Ex-RBI chiefIn an exclusive interview with India Today, former Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor Duvvuri Subbarao said that the rupee's depreciation is a matter of concern, but there is no need to get alarmed about it.

From 4-day work week to higher PF: 11 big changes under the new labour codeThe new labour code may bring some major changes to the workplace, i.e., from work hours and salary structure to gratuity and overtime pay. While some rules could improve long-term savings, others may affect monthly in-hand salary. Here are 11 key changes to know.

Amul, Mother Dairy hike milk prices by Rs 2 per litre nationwide from May 14Your morning tea, coffee or glass of milk may soon feel a little heavier on the pocket. Amul and Mother Dairy have announced a fresh increase in milk prices across India, with the revised rates coming into effect from May 14.

Insurance claim rejected? Reddit user shares how this family fought back and wonMost people stop fighting after an insurance company rejects a claim. One Reddit user, however, shared how a family challenged a rejection linked to a pre-existing condition, spotted an error, escalated the matter, and finally won.

Sensex, Nifty recover after 4-day slide even as rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 49.74 points, or 0.07%, to close at 74,608.98, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 33.05 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,412.60.

RBI Governor says petrol, diesel price hike likely if West Asia crisis drags onRBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said India may have to raise petrol and diesel prices if the West Asia crisis persists. The remarks highlight growing pressure on inflation, fuel imports and state-run oil companies absorbing higher crude costs.

Gold duty hike explained: What happens to your purchases now?Gold prices were already high, and now a fresh duty hike has made them even steeper. With jewellery stores expected to revise prices in the coming days, one big question remains, i.e., how will this impact your next purchase?

Should you buy jewellery stocks now or wait after the customs duty hike?The customs duty hike on gold and silver has rattled jewellery stocks, with concerns rising over weaker demand and higher costs. With shares of major players turning volatile, investors are now asking a key question: is this a buying opportunity or a signal to wait?

Tata Power shares crashed over 6% today. Should you buy?Tata Power shares fell more than 6% after weaker-than-expected March quarter earnings and mixed brokerage calls. The reaction spotlighted valuation concerns even as the company's renewable and solar businesses continued to grow.

Customs duty rises on gold, silver: Will buying slow or stay strong?Gold and silver have turned costlier after the government raised customs duty. The move has immediately sparked questions in bullion markets. Will higher prices cool demand, or will buying continue to stay strong despite the hike?
Sensex opens 100 points higher, Nifty above 23,400; Asian Paints up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 216.67 points to 74,342.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 68.50 points to 23,311.05 as of 9:47 am.

Gold jumps Rs 9,206, silver surges Rs 16,743 after import duty hikeGold futures on MCX jumped Rs 9,206, or 6%, to Rs 1,62,648 per 10 grams in early trade. Silver futures also rallied strongly, rising Rs 16,743, or 6%, to Rs 2,95,805 per kg as of 9 am.

Air India cuts flights to major global destinations as fuel costs surgeAir India has suspended and reduced several international services from June as fuel prices rise and airspace restrictions bite. The cuts underline growing pressure on long-haul operations, with the airline warning that more routes could turn unviable.

From Thailand to Dubai: How your next foreign trip could weaken the rupeeWhat does a Europe honeymoon, Thailand getaway or Dubai shopping spree have in common? Beyond memories and Instagram pictures, they all mean more dollars leaving India. Let's understand here.

Gold, silver imports to get costlier as govt hikes customs duty after PM's appealDays after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for a year amid economic strain caused by the Iran war and pressure on foreign exchange reserves, the government raised import duties on gold and silver from 6 per cent to 15 per cent.

Rising oil prices will impact inflation and interest rates: Nilesh ShahIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Nilesh Shah, Managing Director at Kotak Mahindra Asset Management Company and a member of the Prime Minister's Economic Advisory Council, addressed the recent market downturn and the Prime Minister's call for austerity.

Dubai court acquits man in Dh1.6 million dispute, says failed deal isn't fraudDubai's criminal court acquitted a man accused in an investment-related dispute. The ruling said the case pointed to a civil business fallout, not proven criminal fraud.

Paytm UPI growing twice as fast as UPI industry, here's what's fuelling its comebackIn its Q4 FY26 earnings release filed last week, the company reported that consumer UPI gross transaction value (GTV) grew 46% year-on-year to Rs 5.5 lakh crore in the March quarter, more than double (2.2x) the industry's 21% growth rate.

India has 60 days of crude, no shortage anywhere, says Hardeep Singh PuriSpeaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Hardeep Singh Puri, said India's energy supplies remain secure despite geopolitical uncertainty and military tensions involving major oil-producing regions.

Retail inflation inches up to 3.48% in April, driven by rising food pricesData released by the government on Tuesday showed retail inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), rose to 3.48% in April from 3.40% in March. The reading, however, came in lower than Reuters economists' expectations of 3.8%.

entertainment.headline: Rashmika Mandanna conquers fear of heights in Kochi amid Mysaa shoot Rashmika Mandanna shared glimpses from Kochi and said she faced her fear of heights during the stay. The posts also marked the end of a long Kerala schedule for Mysaa and thanked the crew.
Tara Sutaria channels vintage glamour for Cannes debut Tara Sutaria has begun her Cannes 2026 visit by sharing her first-day pictures from the French Riviera. Take a look at the photos here.

Alia Bhatt brings old-Hollywood glam to Cannes in lavender, choker steals spotlight Alia Bhatt made another appearance at the Cannes Film Festival in a lavender floor-length outfit with noodle straps. Videos and pictures from the outing quickly shifted attention to her choker, which became a talking point online.

Abhishek Bachchan says relationships, not strategy, shape his films In a recent interview, actor Abhishek Bachchan recently spoke about how personal relationships shape the films he accepts. He said comfort, trust and emotional connection matter more to him than strategy.

Lakshmi aa rahi hai: Diljit Dosanjh makes gender reveal memorable for couple Diljit Dosanjh paused his Chicago concert to reveal an expecting couple's baby gender from the stage. The warm exchange drew loud cheers at the arena and quickly spread online.

Celebrity couples, games: Neha Dhupia-Angad Bedi to host new show Double Date Neha Dhupia and Angad Bedi have unveiled the trailer for Double Date, their first show as co-hosts. The series places celebrity duos in relaxed settings designed to spark candid, unscripted conversations.

Rahul Dev calls out paps for insensitive coverage of brother Mukul Dev's funeral Rahul Dev said paparazzi reached Mukul Dev's funeral after he shared details on Instagram for relatives. The episode has sharpened debate over privacy and media sensitivity during moments of grief.

Dhruva Sarja, Sanjay Dutt's KD: The Devil to now release in Telugu. See details The makers of the Kannada film KD: The Devil have announced that the Telugu-dubbed version will release this week. The film stars Dhruva Sarja and Sanjay Dutt.

Tabu says society worries more about women's age than women do Actor Tabu opens up about how women in cinema are judged more harshly for ageing and reflects on the pressures female actors face in the industry. The actor also shares why she never hid her real reasons for rejecting films.

Amid NTR-Neel shoot, Jr NTR to celebrate birthday with family in Hyderabad: Report Jr NTR is expected to celebrate his birthday in Hyderabad this year while taking a short break from NTR-Neel. The visit gains added attention as the film's first glimpse is set to be unveiled on May 20.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram new release date announced Samantha's upcoming Telugu film Maa Inti Bangaram will now release in cinemas worldwide in June. The film was earlier scheduled for a May 15 release.

Billie Eilish's 3D concert film swings between emotional honesty and excessRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Billie Eilish's new concert film Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in3D) pairs her intimate stage persona with James Cameron's expansive 3D style. The result is visually striking but less assured when it reaches for deeper intimacy.

Filmmaker Peter Jackson calls AI 'special effect', warns it will destroy the world Director Peter Jackson said at Cannes that he sees AI in film as just another special effect but warned it could destroy the world.

Jr NTR-Prashanth Neel film's first glimpse to be over 3 minutes long Trade buzz says the first glimpse of Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's NTRNeel may run for more than three minutes when it drops at midnight on May 19.

Fame Gurukul nostalgia hits hard as Arijit Singh-Qazi Touqeer reunite in Jiaganj Singer Qazi Touqeer shared a video revisiting his journey with Arijit Singh, from their Fame Gurukul days to a recent visit to Jiaganj. The clip captures moments from scooty rides around the town to a friendly game of cricket, offering a nostalgic glimpse into their bond.

Ayushmann Khurrana: Pati Patni Aur Woh Do is a pati-verse, not infidelity Ayushmann Khurrana says Pati Patni Aur Woh Do does not glorify cheating and calls it a "comedy of errors". The actor also spoke about comparisons with Sanjeev Kumar and Kartik Aaryan, the film's "pati universe", and his collaboration with Sooraj Barjatya.

Sanjay Gupta explains Dhurandhar gamble, says nobody would bet on Ranveer-Aditya Sanjay Gupta said Aditya Dhar spent four years mounting Dhurandhar before Ranveer Singh and the Ambanis came on board. He also revealed that nobody in the industry was ready to bet on the film given its budget and the phase Ranveer was in.

Don't issue me challan: Rakesh Bedi's comic moment with traffic police goes viral Rakesh Bedi shared a playful moment with traffic police officers who recognised him from Dhurandhar at a traffic signal. The candid exchange drew attention online and highlighted renewed appreciation for his recent roles.

Salaam-e-Ishq sequel in development after nearly 20 years? Here's what we know Nikkhil Advani is reportedly developing Salaam-e-Ishq 2 nearly two decades after the original romantic drama released in theatres. The sequel is expected to follow three couples navigating modern-day relationships and could reportedly launch a larger franchise.

general news.headline: Inside Prateek Yadav's final days: Doctor, postmortem reveal cause of deathPrateek Yadav's doctor said he left the ICU against medical advice despite repeated warnings. His postmortem later attributed his death to massive pulmonary thromboembolism, ruling out steroid speculation.

Cancel NEET, allow Class 12-based admission: CM Vijay reiterates Tamil Nadu stanceAmid the fallout over the cancellation of NEET-UG 2026, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay renewed the state's demand to abolish NEET, calling the paper leak controversy proof of the exam's "structural flaws" and urging medical admissions based on Class 12 marks.

Prateek Yadav dies at 38: A timeline of what transpired before his deathPrateek Yadav's death at a relatively young age left many shocked, especially as he was widely regarded as health conscious. Mourners started gathering outside the family's Vikramaditya Marg residence soon after news of his death spread.

AIADMK sacks CV Shanmugam, others from party posts after split over trust voteThe action came hours after a section of AIADMK MLAs voted in favour of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) government during the trust vote, helping Vijay comfortably prove his majority on the Assembly floor.

Congress attacks Hosabale of RSS for Pak talks stance; Naravane backs Track-2RSS General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale said that "India should always be ready to engage in dialogue with Pakistan". Reacting to it, former Army chief MM Naravane said that he believed in "people-to-people diplomacy". The Congress party has criticised Hosabale for his remark.

At least 27 killed as powerful storm, heavy rain sweep through UP districtsAt least 27 people were killed across Uttar Pradesh after a powerful storm accompanied by heavy rain and strong winds caused widespread destruction in districts including Prayagraj, Bhadohi and Budaun on Wednesday evening.

Karnataka withdraws 2022 uniform order, allows hijab, sacred threads in schoolsUnder the new order, students in government, aided, and private educational institutions, including pre-university colleges, can wear items such as the hijab, sacred thread (janivara), rudraksha, shivadhara, and sharavastra along with the prescribed uniform.

PM Modi isn't asking you to stop spending, but to spend wiselyPrime Minister Narendra Modi asked Indians to cut fuel use, delay gold buys and avoid foreign travel. The Centre said the voluntary appeals were meant to protect foreign exchange reserves, not signal austerity.

How China makes the granddad of NEET leak-proofIndia's national medical entrance exam, NEET-UG, has been embroiled in paper leak and exam centre controversies. While 2.2 million aspirants take the NEET, over 13 million candidates sit for China's Gaokao examination. Here's how China secures Gaokao with security and strict punishment.

Severe heatwave in northwest, heavy rain in northeast on ThursdayThe IMD has forecast monsoon advance over parts of the Bay of Bengal and Andaman Sea around May 16. The update comes as severe heatwave, heavy rain and thunderstorms affect different parts of India.

Live: Trump arrives in China with tech titans, top aides for high-stakes Xi summitTrump in China Live Updates: US President Donald Trump received a grand welcome in Beijing as he began a high-stakes two-day summit. He will meet Chinese President Xi Jinping for talks focused on trade, artificial intelligence and the Iran war. Trump arrived with a delegation that included Elon Musk and Jensen Huang, whose company is seeking approval to sell advanced AI chips in China. Chinese officials, a military honour guard and students waving US and Chinese flags welcomed Trump as he stepped off Air Force One. During the visit, Trump is expected to push Xi to "open up" China further to American businesses while also discussing Taiwan and the ongoing fallout from the Iran war. Trump is also likely to encourage Beijing to help persuade Tehran to reach a deal with Washington and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, although he said he does not need China's help to end the war. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Netanyahu's secret UAE meeting during Iran war comes to lightBenjamin Netanyahu held a previously undisclosed meeting with UAE President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed in Al-Ain during the Israel-Iran war. The disclosure signals deeper Israel-UAE coordination as regional tensions and Iranian attacks intensified.

Alia Bhatt serves Cinderella-coded couture in steel blue gown in CannesAlia Bhatt's second appearance on the Cannes 2026 red carpet was all things dreamy — soft hues, timeless glamour, and just the right touch of drama.

Get its act together: Parliamentary warning to NTA returns after NEET leakThe NEET paper leak has put the NTA under renewed scrutiny as a CBI enquiry begins. The row has revived earlier warnings and reform proposals on exam security, blacklisting and testing infrastructure.

Convoy cuts, metro rides, no Cannes: BJP ministers cut VIP optics, echo PM appealDays after PM Modi urged Indians to embrace Covid-era restraint, BJP-ruled states began sharply reducing the size of ministerial convoys. Several ministers also cancelled foreign trips. Here's a look at the measures announced by different states as part of the austerity drive.

Aparna Yadav posts cremation details after Prateek Yadav's deathAparna Yadav shared her first public reaction after Prateek Yadav's death, announcing his last rites on X. The post came as medical findings confirmed the 38-year-old died of a massive pulmonary thromboembolism.

3,000-km-long band of cloud seen stretching from Kerala to ArunachalSatellite imagery has captured a nearly 3,000-km band of thunderstorms stretching from Kerala to Arunachal Pradesh ahead of the southwest monsoon.

NEET-UG 2026 paper leak: CBI arrests 5 as Opposition steps up attack on BJPThe NEET-UG 2026 exam, held on May 3 for admissions to undergraduate medical courses, was cancelled on Tuesday amid allegations of paper leak. The cancellation has left over 22 lakh medical aspirants and their families in uncertainty.

CM Vijay walks back astrologer-as-OSD move after backlash from allies, criticsAfter facing the wrath of the alliance partners and rivals, CM Vijay decided to cancel the appointment of his astrologer as officer on special duty in his department.

Indian-origin-CEOs trick behind US investor's share-market jackpot?American billionaire investor Michael Milken once joked that whenever a US company appointed an Indian-born CEO, investors should "buy the stock". A 15-year backtest of the theory now claims such a hypothetical portfolio would have delivered annual compounded returns of around 30%, far more than that of the S&P 500.

The internet is finally waking up to the beauty of Odia cuisineOdia cuisine is one of the most underrated, but with time, it is slowly getting its due. The most recent acknowledgement came from industrialist Harsh Goenka.

Punjab grandmother, 61, scores 77% in board exam 2026Narendra Kaur, 61, from Jalandhar has cleared the PSEB Class 10 examination with 500 marks through open schooling. Her result has drawn attention to long-delayed educational aspirations and the reach of second-chance learning.
Bomb-like object recovered near Pune hospital, bomb detection squad deployedAccording to preliminary information, the explosive device was found in the parking area of Kamdhenu Society in the area.

Araghchi arrives in Delhi aboard 'Minab168' plane as Iran war shadows BRICS talksIranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi arrived in New Delhi for the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting chaired by S. Jaishankar. The visit puts the Iran conflict and wider divisions within BRICS at the centre.

sports.headline: Virat Kohli's special night lights up Raipur with plethora of records: In Stats IPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli smashed a sensational century and shattered multiple records as Royal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday in Raipur.

Virat Kohli says he is savouring cricket after record century vs KKR IPL 2026: Virat Kohli struck an unbeaten 105 as RCB beat KKR by six wickets in Raipur. After reaching multiple milestones, he said he now wants to savour every day left in cricket.

Catch of the season? Manish Pandey leaves Virat Kohli, KKR teammates stunned IPL 2026: Manish Pandey pulled off a one-handed diving catch to dismiss Tim David during RCB's chase in Raipur. Virat Kohli's century and RCB's six-wicket win still left KKR facing a tougher qualification path.

Virat Kohli roars back in landmark clash to dent Kolkata's playoffs hopes badly IPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli had a dream day as he scored a hundred and completed 14000 T20 runs as Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat the Kolkata Knight Riders by six wickets on Wednesday in Raipur.

CSK's playoff-push at risk after Jamie Overton flies back to UK due to injury IPL 2026: Jamie Overton will return to the UK after suffering a right thigh injury. His exit leaves Chennai Super Kings without a key all-round option in a crucial playoff phase.

Virat Kohli ends duck streak with animated celebration in RCB vs KKR IPL 2026: Virat Kohli ended his run of ducks with an animated first-run celebration against Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur. The moment capped his record 279th IPL appearance and eased the focus on his brief slump.

Why did Varun Chakravarthy miss the game vs RCB? Head coach Abhishek Nayyar reveals IPL 2026: Abhishek Nayyar said Varun Chakravarthy missed KKR's game against RCB Iin Raipur after suffering a hairline inury. The injury is on his landing foot, leaving the franchise hopeful rather than certain about his return next match.

Sarah Taylor breaks new ground as England men's first-ever female coach Sarah Taylor has become the first woman to join the England men's coaching setup, adding another historic milestone to her trailblazing cricket journey after successful stints with Sussex, Team Abu Dhabi, England Lions and Gujarat Giants.

Real Madrid lose again! This time in court over homophobic chants against Guardiola CAS upheld UEFA's sanctions after Real Madrid challenged punishment over homophobic chants aimed at Pep Guardiola. The ruling strengthens football's harder line on discrimination and leaves Madrid with another off-field defeat.

Venus Williams, 45, returns to French Open after 5 years, set to play doubles French Open 2026: At 45, Venus Williams is set to return to Roland Garros after five years, partnering rising American star Hailey Baptiste in the women's doubles event.

IPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy injury scare grows as KKR spinner misses RCB clash IPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy missed KKR's must-win IPL 2026 match against RCB in Raipur, with Saurabh Dubey replacing him. The late change raised fresh doubts over the spinner's fitness and sharpened the stakes for both teams.

Who is Saurabh Dubey? KKR hand IPL debut to Vidarbha left-arm pacer vs RCBKolkata Knight Riders handed Vidarbha left-arm pacer Saurabh Dubey his IPL debut against Royal Challengers Bengaluru after Varun Chakaravarthy was ruled out with an injury.

Italian Open: Sublime Iga Swiatek steamrolls Pegula to reach semifinalsThree-time Italian Open champion Iga Swiatek dismantled American fifth seed Jessica Pegula 6-1, 6-2 to storm into the semi-finals of the WTA 1000 clay-court tournament in Rome on Wednesday.

Punjab Kings pushback before reset: Bloggers can say anything, we are not concernedIPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: After defending their dressing room against a digital riot of rumours, Punjab Kings look to tap into the calm in Dharamsala to shift focus on their push to the playoffs. Having lost four on the bounce, the home team will take on Mumbai Indians on Thursday.

Hockey India denies PR Sreejesh firing claims, says Olympic hero rejected new roleHockey India has denied PR Sreejesh's claim that he was removed as junior men's coach for a foreign replacement. The exchange has widened a debate over trust in Indian coaches despite his strong results with the side.

Neeraj Chopra gets Switzerland camp approval before CWG and Asian GamesThe Mission Olympic Cell has approved Neeraj Chopra's 47-day training camp in Switzerland and cleared foreign exposure trips for Manu Bhaker and Gulveer Singh. The plans come as India readies key medal contenders for the Commonwealth Games and Asian Games after a demanding season.

Women's T20 World Cup: India to face England, West Indies in warm-up matchesThe ICC has confirmed India's warm-up schedule ahead of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, with Harmanpreet Kaur's side set to face West Indies and hosts England before the main tournament begins.

Ahmedabad set to host FIBA U18 Asia Cup as India's basketball push grows biggerAhmedabad will host the FIBA U18 Asia Cup 2026 from August 13 to 23, with 16 teams competing for the continental title and U19 World Cup places. The event brings a World Cup qualification tournament to India for the first time in 22 years and adds basketball to the city's expanding multi-sport portfolio.

Kagiso Rabada left fascinated by 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's batting approachIPL 2026: Kagiso Rabada has praised 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after the Rajasthan Royals batter attacked Gujarat Titans without hesitation in the IPL. The South African said the teenager's fast hands and fearlessness make him an X-factor while reinforcing the value of orthodox fast bowling in T20 cricket.

Hardik Pandya's absence to continue? MI camp drops hint ahead of PBKS clashMumbai Indians all-rounder Corbin Bosch admitted the team had missed Hardik Pandya's presence, while hinting that the MI captain's availability for the Punjab Kings clash could be decided only shortly before the toss in Dharamsala.

Babar Azam as Test captain again? Pakistan plan sacking Shan Masood after Mirpur lossBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Pakistan are considering bringing back Babar Azam as Test captain after mounting criticism of Shan Masood following the team's 104-run defeat to Bangladesh in the opening Test in Mirpur.

England drop Crawley, Pope as Ben Stokes returns for New Zealand TestsBen Stokes returns to lead England against New Zealand as Zak Crawley and Ollie Pope miss out. The squad signals a clear reset after the Ashes, with three uncapped players and key recalls.

RCB vs KKR Highlights: King Kohli roars in Raipur gets RCB back on top of tableRCB (Bengaluru) vs KKR (Kolkata) Highlights, IPL 2026: RCB (194/4 in 19.1 overs) chasing 192, King Kohli roars in Raipur, scores 9th IPL century to help RCB to a 6-wicket victory over KKR in Raipur.

PR Sreejesh blasts Hockey India, claims he was sacked for a foreign coachPR Sreejesh slammed Hockey India for his removal as head coach for the Indian U-21 hockey team. He alleges that the organisation wanted to appoint a foreigner for the job despite his success with the team.

Jasprit Bumrah stays No.1 Test bowler, Gill and Yashasvi ranked among top 10 battersJasprit Bumrah remains the top-ranked Test bowler in the latest ICC update, while Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shubman Gill stay inside the batting top 10. The rankings underline India's individual quality even as the side waits for its next WTC match after the home defeat to South Africa.

Karnataka all-rounder picked as injury replacement for CSK's Ramakrishna GhoshChennai Super Kings have signed Karnataka all-rounder Macneil Noronha as an injury replacement for Ramakrishna Ghosh as the five-time champions continue their push for an IPL 2026 play-off

Neymar's World Cup call based on fitness and form, not sentiment: Brazil's AncelottiBrazil coach Carlo Ancelotti said Neymar's place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup squad will be decided purely on fitness and form rather than emotion or reputation, as the veteran forward fights to prove he can still meet the demands of elite international football.

AB de Villiers urges Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to test himself across formatsAB de Villiers has urged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not to remain only a T20 specialist batter. He says Test cricket will probe the teenager's game, temperament and resilience far more deeply.

Did Prabhsimran Singh shut down 10kg weight gain rumours? Insta post sparks buzzIPL 2026: Prabhsimran Singh's Instagram story sparked fresh buzz after the Punjab Kings opener appeared to respond to unverified social media rumours surrounding his fitness and the team's recent slump.

Asia's Top Trap Shooter Zoravar Singh dropped from India's Asian Games squadZoravar Singh Sandhu has sought a review after being left out of India's proposed Asian Games trap squad. His omission puts NRAI's rigid selection policy under harsher scrutiny this week.

Messi doubles salary with new Inter Miami deal, set to earn 25 million dollarsLionel Messi's new Inter Miami contract lifts his base salary to $25 million and keeps him miles clear atop MLS. The figures also expose Miami's financial muscle, with the club's payroll well beyond every rival.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
